# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FEATURE FILE 1 — Portfolio Valuation
# Covers: FR-1, FR-2, FR-5
# File: features/portfolio_valuation.feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Feature: Portfolio valuation
  As a portfolio owner
  I want to see the current market value of all my holdings
  So that I know my total net worth at any point in time

  Background:
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user
    And the following companies exist in the database:
      | ticker   | name           | status |
      | DANGCEM  | Dangote Cement | listed |
      | GTCO     | GT Holdco      | listed |
      | OLDMUTUAL| Old Mutual     | defunct|

  Scenario: SC-001 Active holding value is computed from shares and current price
    Given DANGCEM has a current price of "450.00"
    And I hold 100 shares of DANGCEM at an average cost of "400.00"
    When I request GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the response status is 200
    And the holding for DANGCEM has "current_value" equal to "45000.00"
    And the holding for DANGCEM has "cost_basis" equal to "40000.00"
    And the holding for DANGCEM has "return_pct" equal to "+12.50"

  Scenario: SC-002 Claim holding has zero cost basis regardless of purchase price
    Given OLDMUTUAL has holding_type "claim"
    And I hold 500 shares of OLDMUTUAL at an average cost of "5.00"
    When I request GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the holding for OLDMUTUAL has "cost_basis_override" equal to "0.00"
    And the holding for OLDMUTUAL has "return_pct" equal to null

  Scenario: SC-003 Total assets includes both active portfolio and claims value
    Given DANGCEM has a current price of "450.00"
    And I hold 100 shares of DANGCEM with holding_type "active"
    And a ClaimRecord exists for OLDMUTUAL with expected_payout "12000.00" and status "pending"
    When I request GET /api/v1/dashboard
    Then the response contains "active_portfolio_value" equal to "45000.00"
    And the response contains "claims_portfolio_value" equal to "12000.00"
    And the response contains "total_assets" equal to "57000.00"

  Scenario: SC-004 Paid claim uses actual payout not expected payout in total
    Given a ClaimRecord exists with expected_payout "12000.00" and actual_payout "9500.00" and status "paid"
    When I request GET /api/v1/dashboard
    Then the "claims_portfolio_value" equals "9500.00"
    And the "claims_portfolio_value" does not equal "12000.00"

  Scenario: SC-005 Monetary values in API response are strings not floats
    Given DANGCEM has a current price of "450.00"
    And I hold 100 shares of DANGCEM
    When I request GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the field "current_value" in the response is a JSON string
    And the field "current_value" is not a JSON number

  Scenario Outline: SC-006 Holdings filtered by holding_type parameter
    Given holdings exist with types "active" and "claim"
    When I request GET /api/v1/holdings?holding_type=<filter>
    Then the response contains only holdings with holding_type "<expected_type>"

    Examples:
      | filter | expected_type |
      | active | active        |
      | claim  | claim         |

---

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FEATURE FILE 2 — Price Data Entry
# Covers: FR-9
# File: features/price_entry.feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Feature: Price data entry
  As an admin
  I want to update stock prices via manual entry or NGX PDF upload
  So that my portfolio valuations remain current

  Background:
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user
    And ZENITHBANK exists in the database with current_price "31.00"

  Scenario: SC-007 Quick price entry updates current price and writes audit record
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with body:
      | company_id | price  | entry_date |
      | ZENITHBANK | 32.50  | today      |
    Then the response status is 200
    And ZENITHBANK has current_price "32.50" in the database
    And a price_audit record exists with old_price "31.00" and new_price "32.50"
    And the audit record has source "manual"

  Scenario: SC-008 Quick entry rejects a future date
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with entry_date set to tomorrow
    Then the response status is 422
    And the response error contains "Entry date cannot be in the future"
    And ZENITHBANK current_price is unchanged

  Scenario: SC-009 Quick entry rejects a negative price
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with price "-5.00"
    Then the response status is 422
    And the response error contains "Price must be greater than zero"

  Scenario: SC-010 Quick entry rejects price above 100000 naira sanity cap
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with price "100001.00"
    Then the response status is 422
    And the response error contains "100,000 sanity cap"

  Scenario: SC-011 NGX PDF upload updates prices for known tickers
    Given a valid NGX Daily Official List PDF containing:
      | ticker    | close_price |
      | ZENITHBANK| 33.00       |
      | DANGCEM   | 451.00      |
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/upload-pdf with that PDF
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response "updated" count equals 2
    And ZENITHBANK has current_price "33.00"
    And DANGCEM has current_price "451.00"
    And price_audit records exist with source "ngx_pdf_upload" for both tickers

  Scenario: SC-012 NGX PDF upload skips unknown tickers and reports them
    Given a PDF containing ticker "GHOST_TICKER" not in the database
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/upload-pdf with that PDF
    Then the response "skipped" count is at least 1
    And the response "error_details" contains an entry for "GHOST_TICKER"
    And no crash occurs

  Scenario: SC-013 Price revert restores previous price and creates new audit record
    Given a price_audit record exists with id 42, old_price "31.00", new_price "32.50"
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/audit/42/revert
    Then the response status is 200
    And ZENITHBANK has current_price "31.00"
    And a new price_audit record exists with source "revert_of_42"

  Scenario: SC-014 Readonly user cannot update prices
    Given I am authenticated as a readonly user
    When I POST /api/v1/prices/quick with a valid payload
    Then the response status is 403

---

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FEATURE FILE 3 — Authentication
# Covers: NFR-4
# File: features/authentication.feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Feature: Authentication
  As a user
  I want to log in securely and have my session persist for 30 days
  So that I do not need to log in repeatedly for daily use

  Background:
    Given an admin user exists with username "zubbyik"

  Scenario: SC-015 Successful login sets 30-day httpOnly cookie
    When I POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response sets a cookie named "epm_token"
    And the cookie has attribute "HttpOnly"
    And the cookie has attribute "SameSite=Strict"
    And the cookie has Max-Age of 2592000

  Scenario: SC-016 Invalid credentials return 401
    When I POST /api/v1/auth/login with password "wrongpassword"
    Then the response status is 401
    And no cookie is set in the response

  Scenario: SC-017 Authenticated request to /me returns user with role
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user
    When I GET /api/v1/auth/me
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response contains "role" equal to "admin"

  Scenario: SC-018 Logout clears the epm_token cookie
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user
    When I POST /api/v1/auth/logout
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response sets "epm_token" cookie with Max-Age 0

  Scenario: SC-019 Logout is idempotent — returns 200 without a cookie
    Given I am not authenticated
    When I POST /api/v1/auth/logout
    Then the response status is 200

  Scenario: SC-020 Protected route returns 401 without cookie
    Given I am not authenticated
    When I GET /api/v1/holdings
    Then the response status is 401

---

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FEATURE FILE 4 — Dividend Tracking
# Covers: FR-6
# File: features/dividend_tracking.feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Feature: Dividend tracking
  As a portfolio owner
  I want to record dividend payments against each holding
  So that I can track income and prepare for tax filing

  Background:
    Given I am authenticated as an admin user
    And a holding exists for DANGCEM

  Scenario: SC-021 Dividend record stores gross, net, and WHT
    When I POST /api/v1/dividends with:
      | holding_id | gross_amount | wht_rate | payment_date | dividend_type |
      | DANGCEM    | 1000.00      | 0.10     | 2026-04-01   | final         |
    Then the response status is 201
    And the dividend record has "gross_amount" equal to "1000.00"
    And the dividend record has "net_amount" equal to "900.00"
    And the dividend record has "withholding_tax" equal to "100.00"

  Scenario: SC-022 Annual dividend summary aggregates by year
    Given dividend records exist for 2025 totalling gross "5000.00"
    When I GET /api/v1/dividends/summary
    Then the 2025 row has "gross_total" equal to "5000.00"
    And the 2025 row has "wht_total" equal to "500.00"
    And the 2025 row has "net_total" equal to "4500.00"

  Scenario: SC-023 Readonly user can view dividends but not create them
    Given I am authenticated as a readonly user
    When I GET /api/v1/dividends
    Then the response status is 200
    When I POST /api/v1/dividends with a valid payload
    Then the response status is 403

  Scenario: SC-024 WHT rate is configurable per dividend entry
    When I POST /api/v1/dividends with wht_rate "0.075"
    Then the dividend record has "withholding_tax" equal to "75.00"
    And the dividend record has "net_amount" equal to "925.00"

---

# TRACEABILITY MATRIX
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# SC-001  FR-1   current_value = shares × price                → test_holding_value_computed
# SC-002  FR-5   claim holding cost_basis_override = 0        → test_claim_holding_zero_cost
# SC-003  FR-2   total_assets = active + claims                → test_total_assets_includes_claims
# SC-004  FR-2   paid claim uses actual_payout                 → test_paid_claim_uses_actual_payout
# SC-005  FR-8*  monetary values are strings                   → test_monetary_values_are_strings
# SC-006  FR-5   holdings filtered by holding_type             → test_holdings_filter_by_type
# SC-007  FR-9   quick entry updates price + audit             → test_quick_price_entry
# SC-008  FR-9   future date rejected                          → test_price_entry_future_date
# SC-009  FR-9   negative price rejected                       → test_price_entry_negative
# SC-010  FR-9   price > ₦100k rejected                       → test_price_entry_sanity_cap
# SC-011  FR-9   PDF upload updates known tickers              → test_pdf_upload_known_tickers
# SC-012  FR-9   PDF upload skips unknown tickers              → test_pdf_upload_unknown_tickers
# SC-013  FR-9   revert restores previous price                → test_price_revert
# SC-014  NFR-4  readonly cannot write prices                  → test_price_entry_readonly_blocked
# SC-015  NFR-4  login sets 30-day httpOnly cookie             → test_login_sets_cookie
# SC-016  NFR-4  invalid credentials return 401               → test_login_invalid_credentials
# SC-017  NFR-4  /me returns role                              → test_auth_me_returns_role
# SC-018  NFR-4  logout clears cookie                          → test_logout_clears_cookie
# SC-019  NFR-4  logout idempotent                             → test_logout_idempotent
# SC-020  NFR-4  protected route 401 without cookie            → test_protected_route_requires_auth
# SC-021  FR-6   dividend stores gross/net/WHT                 → test_dividend_stores_amounts
# SC-022  FR-6   annual summary aggregates correctly           → test_dividend_annual_summary
# SC-023  FR-6   readonly can view dividends                   → test_dividend_readonly_access
# SC-024  FR-6   WHT rate configurable per entry               → test_dividend_configurable_wht

# Coverage: 24/24 scenarios mapped → 100%
